purpose: Define what the active repository workspace means for agents and harness checks. This prevents generated artifacts, dependency trees, caches, archives, and sibling workspaces from becoming semantic context by accident.
authority_note: This file owns workspace classification and agent read-boundary policy. It does not override product truth, design truth, runtime contracts, or content approval boundaries owned by other specs.

card_make_is_external_content_workspace.claim: The sibling `/Users/lenkin/programing/card make` workspace produces and approves candidate card content. softbook_cet consumes exported payloads, dry-run/imports them, audits coverage, and runs runtime smoke; it does not treat that workspace as internal source.

active_workspace_must_exclude_generated_.claim: Generated outputs, dependency/vendor directories, caches, machine-local files, and inactive archives must not be read as semantic product or implementation authority by default.

active_truth.description: Normative product, delivery, design, and harness truth.

design_authority.description: Accepted design artifacts and design governance documents. Search runs or candidates are evidence, not automatic implementation authority.

runtime_contract.description: Contracts, package metadata, smoke scripts, and deployment notes that describe runtime boundaries.

generated.description: Build products and generated outputs. They may exist locally but are not active semantic context.

dependency_vendor.description: Third-party dependency trees and vendored package outputs. Read package manifests or lockfiles first; do not bulk-read dependency trees.

cache_or_machine_local.description: Machine-local, transient, cache, and editor/runtime byproducts.

archive.description: Inactive historical material. Do not use as active truth unless a task explicitly asks for historical comparison.

external_workspace.description: Sibling or external workspaces referenced by contract but not owned by this repository.

agent_read_policy.default: Start from AGENTS.md, doc-manifest, authority-map, and the task-relevant specs. Do not discover context by recursively reading the whole worktree.
allowed_escalation: explicit user request to inspect generated or dependency artifacts
debug task where the failing artifact itself is the subject
historical comparison task that names archive material
card content handoff task that explicitly crosses into card make

worktree_lifecycle.remote_main_authority: origin/main and GitHub PR merged state are the integration authority; a stale local main worktree is not evidence that a merge failed.
